figures.name: Frumentius
figures.title: Kesate Birhan (Revealer of Light)
figures.quote: A shipwrecked tutor who became the first Abuna of Ethiopia.
figures.summary: Syrian Christian who survived a shipwreck, tutored the future King Ezana, and was consecrated as the first Bishop of Ethiopia by Athanasius.

figures.name: King Ezana
figures.title: The Constantine of Africa
figures.quote: By the power of the Lord of Heaven, who in heaven and upon earth is mightier than everything that exists.
figures.summary: The first Aksumite king to officially embrace Christianity, replacing pagan symbols with the cross on his coinage.

figures.name: Abba Aragawi
figures.title: Founder of Debre Damo
figures.quote: One of the Nine Saints who fled persecution and built a fortress of prayer.
figures.summary: Syrian monk who established the Debre Damo monastery on a sheer-sided amba, accessible only by rope.

figures.name: King Kaleb
figures.title: Defender of the Faith
figures.quote: Launched an amphibious invasion to avenge the martyrs of Najran.
figures.summary: Aksumite king who conquered Yemen to protect persecuted Christians, later retiring to a monastery and sending his crown to Jerusalem.

figures.name: Al-Najashi (King Armah)
figures.title: Protector of the First Hijra
figures.quote: A king who rules without injustice.
figures.summary: Aksumite king who provided refuge to early Muslims fleeing persecution in Mecca, an act of profound hospitality.
